title: sabulina howellii
intro: Sabulina howellii, also known as Howell's saxifrage, is a flowering plant species native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Sabulina howellii often include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various ailments.
